target_users: Developers managing multiple local development projects from one dashboard
key_features: One-click app start and stop controls
Mixed app support for web apps, CLI tools, Python scripts, Docker services, and static apps
Configuration-driven app definitions with local overrides
Path expansion and click-to-open project folders
Config refresh and validation API endpoints
React dashboard with real-time status indicators
Kanban-style persistent card ordering with mouse and keyboard reordering
Command palette and global keyboard shortcuts
Theme, filter, sort, density, favorites, toast, and activity-log dashboard affordances
Detached single-instance launcher script for backend and frontend reuse
risks: Backend process state is in-memory, so running app status resets when the backend process restarts
App start/open-terminal flows may still open Terminal windows for launched child projects depending on app configuration
Governance harness verify currently spends more than the tool timeout during downstream stamp scanning, requiring targeted verification evidence until the harness scan is optimized
The governance repository itself has uncommitted/untracked governance files that need Douglas-owned PR handling under the read-only write boundary

_evidence.project_name: FLOYD.md:1 names '# dev-launcher — FLOYD.md'; FLOYD.md:7 path '/Volumes/Storage/Development/dev-launcher'
_evidence.completion_percentage: Feature inventory method: README.md:11-18,153-163,165-177 claim launcher, port management, mixed apps, path handling, configuration, process management, validation/refresh; backend/server.js routes at ast_grep lines 167,181,293,317,330,344,361,491,500,511,536 implement APIs; frontend/src/App.jsx grep lines 273,286,302,312,491,711,769,857,868,891 implement dashboard controls. 13 of 15 claimed/active feature groups are implemented and verified; known gaps are documentation port drift and backend in-memory process persistence, yielding 87%.
_evidence.tech_stack: backend/package.json:2-6 identifies dev-launcher-backend, ESM Node entry; backend/package.json:21-23 lists express and cors; frontend/package.json:13-20 lists React, lucide-react, Tailwind-related deps; frontend/package.json:22-35 lists ESLint, Playwright, Vite tooling; FLOYD.md:70-75 records JavaScript, Node.js, Express, React 19 + Vite, and ports.
_evidence.complexity_score: Rubric: source files found under backend/frontend/scripts = 18 (simple/moderate boundary); dependencies = backend 2 runtime + frontend 7 runtime + 13 dev = 22 (moderate); architecture = two-service Express + Vite dashboard (moderate); state = in-memory + localStorage/filesystem (moderate); auth = none (simple); concurrency = spawned child processes and dev servers (moderate); data = flat JS config/localStorage (simple). Average approx 4.3, ceiling 5.

_evidence.go_to_market_timeline: repository-report-spec.md formula: completion 70-90% -> '2-4 weeks'; completion_percentage is 87.
_evidence.industry_vertical: README.md:3 says web-based application launcher for development projects; backend/package.json:13-19 keywords include development, launcher, dev-tools, nodejs, frontend, backend.
_evidence.business_model: README.md:3 frames the tool as a local launcher for 'your development projects'; FLOYD.md:69 describes a local development project dashboard; no public pricing or customer packaging evidence was found, so Internal Tool is the supported classification.
_evidence.technical_debt: grep for TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/UNVERIFIED returned no matches; Issues/dev-launcher_ISSUES.md:42 records ISSUE-0003 as verified after forbidden-port docs were corrected; governance checks found missing repository report before this file was created and uncommitted governance-layer drift. Score 24 reflects low code-marker debt with moderate governance process debt.
_evidence.scalability_needs: FLOYD.md:69 says local development launcher; backend/server.js maintains runningApps in memory and spawns local processes; needs are medium because it coordinates multiple local apps/processes but has no database or distributed runtime.
_evidence.target_users: README.md:3 explicitly targets development projects; README.md:69 says view and launch all your apps from the dashboard; FLOYD.md:69 says managing and running local development projects from a single dashboard.
_evidence.key_features: README.md:11-18,153-163,165-177 plus backend/server.js ast_grep route evidence and frontend/src/App.jsx grep evidence for implemented controls, kanban board, toolbar, status bar, toasts, command palette, and launcher behavior.
_evidence.risks: Issues/dev-launcher_ISSUES.md:42 records forbidden default-port documentation as verified fixed; FLOYD.md:71-72 and backend/server.js process map behavior show no database and in-process state; final governance checks showed governance repo dirty and governance-bump verify timed out during downstream scanning.

_critic_notes: Round 1 self-critic: replaced unsupported launch-readiness optimism with an 87% feature-inventory score and explicit risks.
Round 2 code evidence audit: tied each field to read/grep/ast_grep evidence and avoided inferred dependencies not present in package manifests.
Round 3 fresh-eyes review: confirmed no field is placeholder or generic; retained governance harness timeout and dirty governance worktree as explicit risks instead of hiding them.
